skill.name: daily-news-report
skill.description: Scrapes content based on a preset URL list, filters high-quality technical information, and generates daily Markdown reports.
skill.summary: Automates daily news aggregation from multiple tech sources into curated Markdown reports
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate news aggregation tool that scrapes public websites and generates markdown reports. Content hashing uses MD5 for deduplication (not security), backticks are markdown code fences (not shell execution), hardcoded URLs are the core functionality, and environment checks are fallback mechanisms.

medium_findings.title: External Command Pattern in Documentation
medium_findings.description: The skill uses backtick syntax in documentation. This is markdown code fence formatting, not actual shell command execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks are markdown code fences in documentation, not backtick command execution in code.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Configuration
low_findings.description: The skill contains hardcoded URLs to news sources. These are legitimate public websites being scraped as the core functionality.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These URLs are the primary data sources for the news aggregator - Hacker News, HuggingFace, Paul Graham Essays are all well-known public tech blogs.

low_findings.title: Content Hashing for Deduplication
low_findings.description: The skill uses MD5 hashing for content deduplication. This is appropriate for content fingerprinting, not security.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: MD5 is used for content deduplication fingerprints, not security encryption. This is a legitimate use case.

low_findings.title: Environment Detection for Compatibility
low_findings.description: The skill checks for sub-agent availability as part of fallback mechanism.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: This is a legitimate compatibility check to detect available sub-agents and gracefully degrade to serial execution if needed.

content.value_statement: This skill automates gathering and curating daily tech news from multiple sources into a single organized Markdown report, saving hours of manual research time.
actual_capabilities: Scrapes content from 10+ curated tech news sources (Hacker News, HuggingFace Papers, Paul Graham Essays, etc.)
Filters and prioritizes high-quality technical content using quality scoring
Automatically deduplicates content using URL and title similarity checks
Generates well-formatted Markdown reports with summaries and key points
Supports parallel execution with sub-agents and graceful fallback to serial mode
Maintains cache to avoid re-processing previously covered articles
limitations: Depends on external news sources being accessible and not rate-limited
Browser rendering required for JavaScript-heavy sites (Product Hunt, Latent Space)
Quality of output depends on source availability and content freshness
No built-in support for custom source configuration through user prompts

use_cases.title: Daily Research Brief for Developers
use_cases.description: Software developers use this skill each morning to get a curated digest of the top tech news and articles without manually visiting multiple sites.

use_cases.title: Team Knowledge Sharing
use_cases.description: Team leads generate daily reports to share with their team, keeping everyone updated on the latest tech trends and tools.

use_cases.title: Content Curation for Newsletters
use_cases.description: Content creators use the generated reports as source material for weekly tech newsletters or blog posts.
use_cases.target_user: Tech content creators and newsletter authors

prompt_templates.title: Basic Daily Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the daily-news-report skill to generate today's tech news report.
prompt_templates.scenario: Get a quick daily digest of tech news

prompt_templates.title: Specific Date Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the daily-news-report skill with the argument [optional: 2026-02-20] to generate a report for a specific date.
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate a report for a past date

prompt_templates.title: Filtered by Topic
prompt_templates.prompt: Run daily-news-report skill and prioritize content related to AI and machine learning in the final report.
prompt_templates.scenario: Get news focused on a specific topic

prompt_templates.title: Full Multi-Source Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute daily-news-report with all available sources including browser-based sources to generate a comprehensive report.
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate the most comprehensive report possible

output_examples.output: Generates a Markdown file in NewsReport/ directory containing: Title with date, statistical summary (sources count, items collected), 20 high-quality items each with summary, key points, source link, keywords, and quality score.
Produces '2026-02-15-news-report.md' with news from that specific date, including only articles not in the cache history.

best_practices: Run the skill early in the day when source websites have fresh content
Review the quality scores to understand how content was filtered
Check the cache to avoid duplicating content from previous reports
anti_patterns: Do not run the skill multiple times in one day - it will only produce duplicate content
Do not expect real-time news - there is a delay from publication to scraping
Do not rely on this for breaking news - it is designed for curated daily digests

faq.question: What sources does this skill scrape?
faq.answer: The skill scrapes from Hacker News, HuggingFace Papers, Paul Graham Essays, One Useful Thing, James Clear, Farnam Street, HackerNoon, Scott Young, Product Hunt, and Latent Space.

faq.question: How long does it take to generate a report?
faq.answer: Depending on source availability, it takes approximately 2-6 minutes. Tier 1 sources alone take about 2 minutes.

faq.question: Can I add my own news sources?
faq.answer: Currently, source configuration requires editing the sources.json file directly. Custom source addition is not supported through prompts.

faq.question: What happens if a source is unavailable?
faq.answer: The skill has fault tolerance - if one source fails, it continues with remaining sources and logs the error. Failed sources are marked in the configuration.

faq.question: Does the skill work without internet?
faq.answer: No, the skill requires internet access to scrape news sources. It will fail if external websites are unreachable.

faq.question: How does the quality scoring work?
faq.answer: Each source has an average quality score (3.8-4.8). Articles are scored based on source credibility, engagement metrics, and content relevance to tech topics.
